paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement is addressed to all UK Solvency II firms which write with-profits insurance business and which are therefore required to calculate surplus funds. It sets out the Prudential
In particular, this statement sets out the PRA’s expectations regarding the following topics:

This statement expands on the PRA’s general approach as set out in its insurance approach document.1 By clearly and consistently explaining its expectations of firms in relation to the particular area
This statement has been subject to public consultation2 and reflects the feedback that was received by the PRA.

sections.chapter_title: Interaction of the surplus funds calculation with the calculation of technical provisions

paragraphs.summary: Technical Provisions 9.1(3) in the PRA Rulebook requires a firm to include, when calculating its technical provisions, all payments

The PRA expects that surplus funds will normally meet the criteria for classification as Tier 1 own funds as referred to in paragraph [1.5](/guidance/supervisory-statements/ss13-15---solvency-ii-surpl

The calculation of surplus funds in the Surplus Funds Part does not refer to or include a risk margin. The risk margin is a Solvency II requirement in respect of technical provisions and does not ther
Surplus Funds 3.2 generally requires the value of with-profits policy liabilities to be calculated on the basis of a retrospective approach

Calculations of with-profits policy liabilities are determined on the basis of the aggregate value in respect of each with-profits policy. For this purpose calculations may be based on groupings of si
The PRA expects firms to ensure that the choice of groupings is appropriate having regard to (a) to (c) above each time it calculates surplus funds.
The reference to ‘permanent enhancements’ within Surplus Funds 3.3(3) is intended to capture amounts previously added to with-profits polic
The reference to ‘miscellaneous surplus’ within Surplus Funds 3.3(4) is intended to capture surplus or deficit arising from the experience
Surplus Funds 3.5 specifies the extent to which future discretionary additions to guaranteed benefits and discretionary payments should be
